sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
sources.clinical_setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for non-judgemental adherence history, missed-dose or medicine-taking behaviour documentation, beliefs, concerns, practical barriers, preferred support, and periodic review. It does not recommend a medicine, dose, change, or adherence intervention automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.2 — periodic review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.

archive.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
archive.population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
archive.setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.2 — periodic review
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
sources.clinical_setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.

exact_sections.heading: Medicines-related communication between care settings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

exact_sections.heading: Medicines reconciliation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

exact_sections.heading: Medication review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.3.7 — transfer information and medicines reconciliation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
